channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Inside Trello’s reinvention as a personal tasks app
item.description: Riddle me this: What exactly is Trello? Despite counting myself as a heavy-duty power user of the product for well over a decade now, it’s a question I’ve long struggled to answer.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: Photoshop gets its most powerful free iPhone app so far – with a mix of classic tools and Firefly AI
item.description: Adobe has launched a new Photoshop app for iPhones – and it's the closest thing we've seen to a mobile version of the powerful desktop app.
item.dc:creator: mark.wilson@futurenet.com (Mark Wilson), TechRadar

item.title: Massive botnet is targeting Microsoft 365 accounts across the world
item.description: A new password spraying attack was spotted being operated by a huge botnet, targeting Microsoft 365 accounts.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: El plan de transformación digital del Gobierno de Navarra: de la gestión de datos a la capacitación
item.description: En el marco de su plan de transformación digital, el Gobierno de Navarra buscaba una solución integral para gestionar sus datos de manera eficiente y segura.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google Messages will soon get a big upgrade for photo and video quality – and I’m going to use this a lot
item.description: Google Messages is making a change to its camera and gallery functions that should make it much easier to send photos and videos to your friends and family in their original quality.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: How VR technology is curing loneliness in seniors
item.description: VR transforms senior care: Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says immersive tech helps older adults explore and connect via virtual experiences.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Potential cuts at AI Safety Institute stoke concerns in tech industry
item.description: Potential cuts to the U.S. AI Safety Institute (AISI) are causing alarm among some in the technology space who fear the development of responsible artificial intelligence (AI) could be at risk as President Trump works to downsize the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Prosegur despliega la IA generativa en el puesto de trabajo de Iberia y Latam
item.description: Prosegur apuesta por la transformación digital del puesto de trabajo de la mano de Telefónica en sus filiales de la Península Ibérica y Latinoamérica.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: CIO hiring on the rise: How to land a top tech exec role in 2025
item.description: Early returns on 2025 hiring for IT leaders suggest a robust market. For some recruitment firms, job growth for tech executive positions is at great heights.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: What the private sector can teach government about cloud computing
item.description: Cloud computing has been at the forefront of government IT strategies for many years. When cloud computing first became a thing in 2008, the government led the way, including NIST creating a definition of cloud computing. I worked closely with them then and watched the whole thing play out.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: What to know about Apple’s biggest-ever U.S. investment
item.description: This morning, Apple announced its largest spend commitment to date: A whopping $500 billion that’s set to be invested in American manufacturing, engineering, and AI efforts over the next four years.
item.dc:creator: Grace Snelling, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft cancels data center lease plans, raising questions
item.description: Microsoft is slowing down or possibly redirecting its data center construction plans, according to a report by TD Cowen. Analysts with the brokerage say its a sign of the company “potentially being in an oversupply position.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: North Korean hackers behind $1.5 billion crypto hack: Security firm
item.description: North Korean hackers were behind a sophisticated hack that stole about $1.5 billion worth of digital currency from Bybit, a major cryptocurrency exchange, according to security firm TRM.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: How agentic AI will shape the future of business
item.description: In 2024, Amazon introduced its AI-powered HR assistant, which helps managers with performance reviews and workforce planning. Similarly, Tesla deployed AI personas to assist in real-time production monitoring and supply chain optimization.
item.dc:creator: Faisal Hoque, Fast Company

item.title: Top Apple exec concedes he had doubts about app store fees imposed on alternative payment options
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Longtime Apple executive Phil Schiller on Monday conceded he initially opposed fees that the iPhone maker charges for in app-transactions processed outside its own payment system because he feared violating a court-ordered mandate designed to create more competition.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal News Network’s DoD Modernization Exchange 2025
item.description: Hear from top Defense and industry leaders on the latest tech, strategy and innovation shaping DoD modernization The post Federal News Network’s DoD Modernization Exchange 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: The rise of the TV monitor: MSI joins the likes of Samsung and LG with a smart monitor that offers Google TV and even a remote control
item.description: MSI enters smart monitor market with 27-inch Modern MD272UPSW, blending 4K UHD visuals with Google TV.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Apple planning to invest $500B in US, hire 20K people
item.description: Apple announced Monday that it plans to spend more than $500 billion in the United States and hire more than 20,000 people over the next several years.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: This is what no one planned could happen after taking a DNA test
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says DNA tests pose privacy risks like shared genetic information, inaccuracies and hard data deletion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple pledges to spend $500 billon in the US over the next few years
item.description: Apple will invest the amount of revenue it makes in an entire year by 2029 to support US manufacturing and R&D.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Brazil Supreme Court justice orders Rumble suspension nationwide for alleged non-compliance
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — A Brazilian Supreme Court justice on Friday ordered the suspension of video-sharing platform Rumble in the South American nation for allegedly refusing to comply with court orders.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sweden is investigating a damaged cable in the Baltic Sea
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Swedish authorities said on Friday they were investigating a damaged cable that was discovered in the Baltic Sea, the latest in a string of recent incidents of ruptured undersea cables that have heightened fears of Russian sabotage and spying in the region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Apple drops encryption feature for UK users after government reportedly demanded backdoor access
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple said Friday it will stop offering an advanced data security option for British users after the government reportedly demanded that the company provide backdoor access for any data those users have stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Deno update brings OpenTelemetry integration
item.description: Deno Land has released Deno 2.2, a new version of the Deno runtime for JavaScript and TypeScript that features built-in OpenTelemetry for monitoring logs, metrics, and traces. The new Deno release also features Linter updates that include a new plugin system and new rules for React and Preact users.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Chaos ahead: emerging technologies will test data privacy, says Britain’s ICO
item.description: Emerging technologies such as connected transport, quantum sensing, healthcare diagnostics, and synthetic AI-driven content are creating huge gaps in data protection and privacy that could quickly outpace the ability of regulators and enterprises to contain them.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: OSINT Unveiled: The Technology Behind the Intelligence – ODNI Edition
item.description: Learn how OSINT is shaping the future of intelligence and security. The post OSINT Unveiled: The Technology Behind the Intelligence – ODNI Edition first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Google’s new AI co-scientist aims to speed up the scientific discovery process
item.description: Google has unveiled an AI co-scientist built on its Gemini 2.0 platform, aiming to accelerate scientific research by generating novel hypotheses and refining experiments. While the system shows promise, questions remain about its performance and broader applicability.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
